commands.repair_reason: structured ClawSweeper needs-human verdict with repairable P-severity findings (sha=ce615a6646ddac5a005e08dc35d576dfa234f5de)
commands.review_summary:
The PR reclassifies cached Claude OAuth credentials as Claude CLI-owned when Claude CLI file or keychain storage is detected, and adds regression tests for file-backed, delegated-refresh, and keychain-backed ownership.

Reproducibility: yes. from source inspection: current main direct-refreshes expired `.codexbar` cache records, while the linked issue describes how that can consume Claude CLI's rotating refresh token. I did not run a live Claude CLI/keychain reproduction because repository policy avoids real keychain probes without explicit request.
commands.review_followup: - [P1] The remaining blocker mixes a narrow code defect with contributor-only real behavior proof, so this should stay in human PR review rather than a repair lane.
